channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Supreme Court stays Madras HC order restraining TVK MLA from Assembly participation
item.description: The Supreme Court of India on Wednesday stayed a Madras High Court order that had restrained Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) MLA-elect Sreenivasa Sethupathi from participating in the Tamil Nadu Assembl...

item.title: UK to contribute military assets to multinational Strait of Hormuz security mission
item.description: The United Kingdom has committed fighter jets, drones, and a warship to a multinational mission designed to protect shipping in the Strait of Hormuz. Defence Minister John Healey announced the plan du...

item.title: Speculative Scenario Suggests Trump Could Be Removed Under 25th Amendment
item.description: In a recent opinion piece, Robert Reich shared a conversation with political insiders who speculated about a potential scenario in which former President Donald Trump could be removed from office thro...

item.title: Weekly Overview of Nuclear and Global Energy Developments
item.description: This edition of non-corporate nuclear news provides a comprehensive update on global nuclear issues, energy trends, and geopolitical tensions. Key stories highlight the shift of India toward green ene...

item.title: Senegalese Leaders Faye and Sonko Clash Over Political Control and Succession
item.description: In Senegal, what started as a celebrated political alliance between President Bassirou Diomaye Faye and Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko is now showing signs of serious tension. The lead-up to a big rally...

item.title: Tanzania to expand universal health insurance with Sh150,000 annual package for households
item.description: The Tanzanian government is putting the final touches on reforms to the Universal Health Insurance (UHI) system. The new phase introduces a Sh150,000 yearly insurance package for households of up to s...

item.title: Ongoing Developments in the Middle East Conflict on May 13, 2026
item.description: As of May 13, 2026, tensions and conflict continue in the Middle East, with reports highlighting developments involving Iran, Saudi Arabia, and the United Arab Emirates. The situation remains fluid, w...

item.title: US FDA Chief Marty Makary Steps Down Amid Controversies
item.description: President Donald Trump don announce say Marty Makary, wey be US Food and Drug Administration (FDA) chief, don resign from him post. Dis one happen after some weeks wey the agency dey face political wa...

item.title: Trump Set to Discuss Trade and Middle East Issues During Beijing Visit
item.description: US President Donald Trump is travelling to Beijing for a significant summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping, aiming to discuss trade, Taiwan, and the ongoing Middle East conflict. Emphasising busine...

item.title: Property investment expected to rise slightly before negative gearing restrictions take effect
item.description: Ahead of significant tax changes set for July 2027, the Australian property market is likely to see a modest rise in investor activity. The federal Budget announced that negative gearing will be restr...

item.title: Essential Beauty Products and Trends for Spring and Summer 2026
item.description: As the seasons transition from spring to summer, keeping your skincare and beauty routine updated is key. This article from The Curator highlights essential products to achieve a fresh, radiant look f...

item.title: Tanzania Integrates Climate Resilience into National Development Plans
item.description: Dar es Salaam — Tanzania is now putting environmental conservation and climate resilience at the heart of its long-term development strategy, as outlined in Vision 2050 and the Long-Term Perspective P...

item.title: South Coast Railway Zone to Start Operations from Visakhapatnam on June 1
item.description: The South Coast Railway (SCoR) zone, with Visakhapatnam as its headquarters, is set to begin operations from June 1, 2026. This milestone is being celebrated as the culmination of a sustained 40-year ...

item.title: Yanga supporters raise over Sh31 million to cover TPLB fine after derby breach
item.description: Members and supporters of Young Africans (Yanga) football club in Tanzania have shown strong unity and financial power after raising Sh31,210,840 to pay a Sh30 million penalty imposed by the Tanzania ...

item.title: Alexa Ilacad expresses gratitude to fans as KDLex partnership concludes
item.description: Filipino actress and singer Alexa Ilacad has expressed her heartfelt gratitude to her fans following the official conclusion of the popular KDLex love team with singer and actor KD Estrada. Over the y...

item.title: Starmer schedules discussion with Streeting prior to King's address
item.description: Labour Party leader Keir Starmer is reported to be arranging a meeting with fellow party member Wes Streeting in advance of the King's forthcoming speech. The discussion is expected to focus on key is...

item.title: British-Nigerian man escapes UK prison due to administrative error
item.description: One British-Nigerian man, Ifedayo Adeyeye, wey dey serve jail sentence for abducting him pikin, don escape UK after dem release am by mistake. Court hearing on Monday show say HMP Pentonville no notif...

item.title: Former GOP Analyst Warns Trump Policies May Harm Republicans Ahead of 2026 Midterms
item.description: Former GOP strategist Rick Wilson, co-founder of The Lincoln Project, has raised concerns about the Republican Party's strategy under former President Donald Trump as the 2026 midterms approach. Wilso...

item.title: NBI Director claims Senator Dela Rosa evaded arrest by pushing agents
item.description: National Bureau of Investigation (NBI) Director Melvin Matibag reported that Senator Ronald 'Bato' Dela Rosa pushed two female NBI agents who were attempting to serve him with an International Crimina...

item.title: Senator Questions Defense Secretary Hegseth on Military Priorities Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: During a Senate Appropriations Committee hearing, Senator Chris Coons of Delaware directly challenged Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth, expressing concern that Hegseth was prioritizing culture war issue...
